title: Offset Phrase Reveal
prompt: Create a production-ready Offset Phrase Reveal using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original text effect reveals a three-part editorial phrase one phrase at a time when a reader activates a clear control. Use a white 22px-radius Bento card, #111111 headline, #4A4A4F explanation, #ECECEE separation, and a single violet underline accent; never put a gradient on the card or apply low-contrast decorative type. The initial phrase should be useful in server rendering, with a Show next phrase button that cycles through deliberate copy. On click, Enter, or Space, offset the old phrase upward and bring the new phrase from below using transform and opacity only; use 260ms cubic-bezier(.22,1,.36,1) and a subtle spring near stiffness 300 damping 27 for the underline. Keep state local, do not use ambient looping, random values, storage, network calls, remote assets, or timers. Use semantic heading and button markup, an aria-live polite region for the changed phrase, a visible focus ring, 44px touch target, and AA color contrast. The text must wrap cleanly from 320px compact cards through larger detail previews, with clamp typography and no fixed positioning. With prefers-reduced-motion, swap the phrase with a 120ms opacity fade and preserve the final readable state. Return complete paste-ready client code plus a pure CSS approximation with suggested HTML.

code.css:
/* HTML:

Make room for focus.

*/ .offset-phrase-reveal{max-width:30rem;padding:24px;border:1px solid #ececee;border-radius:22px;background:#fff;color:#111}.offset-phrase-reveal h2{font-size:clamp(1.7rem,7vw,2.4rem);line-height:1.05}.offset-phrase-reveal h2::after{display:block;width:42%;height:4px;margin-top:16px;border-radius:999px;background:#a78bfa;content:""}.offset-phrase-reveal button{min-height:44px;padding:0 16px;border:0;border-radius:12px;background:#111;color:#fff;font-weight:800}.offset-phrase-reveal button:focus-visible{outline:3px solid #111;outline-offset:3px}
